text: Tissue healing following childbirth is understood to take 4-6 months, and these timescales should be considered before returning to running postnatally.
Running involves sudden rises in intra-abdominal pressure and considerable force transmission through the lower limbs and pelvis, with potential contre coup effects within the uterus.
Returning to running too soon after childbirth may increase the risk of pelvic floor dysfunction.
Many women continue to run throughout their pregnancies, and medical, health and fitness professionals should be prepared to support their postnatal return to running.
